title: Pen Track
desc: One of the warbeast pen gates is grinding in its track again, which is exactly the sort of warning nobody here ignores.

steps.text:
The warbeast pens in the lower catacombs are built around one guiding principle: anything large enough to bite through stone should never be delayed by faulty hardware unless the delay is happening on the far side of the gate. The gate track outside pen four disagrees with that principle today. Sand and scale grit have packed into the lower groove, and the sliding slab now complains every time the handler opens it for cleaning or inspection.

The pen mechanic keeps one eye on the gate and the other on the feed hatch. "Clear the lower track if you want the blunt work," she says. "Or replace the scent braid at the hatch if you would rather keep your hands farther from the door. Both help. One fixes the gate. The other improves the mood of whatever is waiting behind it."

options.label: Clear the grit from the lower gate track
Replace the calming scent braid at the feed hatch

steps.text:
The gate groove is packed with a mean little mixture of sand, claw scrapings, and scale dust polished hard by repeated weight. You scrape it out section by section, brush the channel clean, and work the slab back and forth until the dragging grind softens into the heavy controlled slide the mechanic had wanted in the first place. The difference is audible before it is visible. The gate no longer sounds offended by its own existence.

From behind the stone barrier comes one low dissatisfied rumble, then silence, as if the occupant has decided the improved hardware is acceptable for now. The mechanic tests the slide once more and seems happier with the result than she is willing to admit outright. "Good. If a pen gate must move, let it move with confidence."

options.label: Brush the last grit into the waste pan and clear the lane

steps.text:
The scent braid hangs beside the feed hatch where the warm air from the pen catches it and carries the calming blend inward. Today's braid has dried brittle and lost most of its effect, which explains the impatient scraping from the other side of the wall. You unhook the old length, knot a fresh braid of floral fibers and mineral leaves in its place, and test the airflow until the new scent begins threading through the slit properly.

The scraping inside the pen eases after a few breaths. Not trust, exactly. More like temporary professional courtesy. The mechanic watches the hatch with her arms folded, then nods once when the chamber remains quiet. "Better. Everyone behaves more sensibly when the air reminds them to."

options.label: Hang the stale braid in the discard basket and step away
